primary_accounting_issue: Déterminer si le revenu lié à une licence de propriété intellectuelle doit être comptabilisé selon le modèle du droit d’accès ou selon le modèle du droit d’utilisation, ce qui détermine une reconnaissance étalée dans le temps ou à une date donnée.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: Applying the precedence rule strictly, IFRS supersedes IAS and IFRIC supersedes SIC; here only IFRS 15 addresses the revenue recognition model for licences of intellectual property, so it is the single governing lineage.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the context includes direct IFRS 15 licence-specific guidance that expressly determines whether revenue is recognised over time or at a point in time.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern the revenue recognition model for licences of intellectual property and distinguish the two licence-specific recognition models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs provide the general IFRS 15 control and timing framework that the licence guidance applies and maps into over-time versus point-in-time recognition.
These paragraphs support the measurement of progress only after the over-time licence model has been selected.
These paragraphs clarify how to assess whether a licence conveys a right to access or a right to use and exclude irrelevant factors.
These paragraphs provide a royalty-specific overlay that modifies timing within the licence model but is not a separate peer top-level approach.

peripheral_authority.reason: The real-estate over-time guidance is topically related to timing of revenue but not relevant to identifying peer approaches for licences of intellectual property.
These transaction price, variable consideration, financing, non-cash consideration and consideration payable rules are not needed to identify the peer licence recognition models.
Principal-versus-agent guidance concerns gross versus net presentation, not the peer licence timing models.
The tuition-fee agenda decision concerns a different fact pattern and does not contribute to identifying licence-specific peer approaches.

treatment_families.family: Licence-specific intellectual property revenue recognition models

approaches.label: Right to access intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats a distinct licence as a right to access intellectual property when the criteria in paragraph B58 are met, with revenue recognised over time.

approaches.label: Right to use intellectual property
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 15 treats a distinct licence as a right to use intellectual property when the paragraph B58 criteria are not met, with revenue recognised at a point in time.
